nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: und
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of   Runte  .

variants.name: Runte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Erhard.

German: in the northwest of Germany most probably a topographic name from a field name based on run ‘mud’, which gave rise to several placenames in the Rhine-Ruhr area, for example Runten, Runtenberg, Rünthe near Hamm, Westphalia.

nameMeanings.name: von

meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Mörle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Etienne, Henri, Jean-Paul.

French, English (of Norman origin), German, and Dutch: nickname, possibly for a good or habitual singer or whistler, from Old French, Middle High German merle ‘blackbird’ (from Latin merula). See also Italian and Spanish Merlo .
French: habitational name from (Le) Merle, the name of several places in various parts of France.

variants.name: Merlo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Mario, Arturo, Jesus, Luis, Raul, Adan, Adela, Agustin, Azucena, Baldemar, Dalila.  Italian Aldo, Angelo, Carlo, Santo, Vito, Antonio, Carmela, Elvio, Enrico, Franco, Grazia, Mauro.

Italian and Spanish: nickname from Italian and medieval Spanish merlo ‘blackbird’ (from Latin merula). In the Middle Ages this bird seems to have been regarded at times as foolish and gullible, and at other times as cunning and wily. In some cases the surname may have arisen as a metonymic occupational name for a bird catcher. See also Merle 1.

variants.name: Marleau
meanings: Altered form of French Merlot: derivative of Merle ‘blackbird’. Compare Marlow and Marlowe .

variants.name: Lemelle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine.
French: variant of Lemesle, itself an altered (assimilated) form of Lemerle, a nickname from le merle ‘the blackbird’ (see Merle ).

variants.name: Merlet
meanings: French: derivative of merle ‘blackbird’ (see Merle ). Compare Marlette .

History: Gédéon Merlet/Marlet from Roussy-le-Village in Moselle, France, married Margriet Martijn in Leiden, the Netherlands, in 1644, died in Piscataway, NJ, c. 1682. In 1662 they emigrated to the Dutch colony of New Amsterdam in New Netherland (now New York City, NY). Gédéon's name is listed (as Gidéon Merlet) in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors.

nameMeanings.name: Irmgard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from erm(en) ‘whole, entire’ + gard ‘enclosure’.

variants.name: Irmengard
meanings: Variant of   Ermengard .

variants.name: Irmingard
meanings: from erm(en) ‘whole, entire’ + gard ‘enclosure’.

variants.name: Ermgard
meanings: from erm(en) ‘whole, entire’ + gard ‘enclosure’.

en.lifeSummary: When Irmgard von Kleeberg und von Mörle was born in 1190, her father, Siegfried II von Büdingen, Graf von Mörle und Kleeberg, was 26 and her mother, Euphemia von Staden, was 22. She married Heinrich I Isenburg von Limburg in 1210. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. She died in 1220, in Grenzau, Höhr-Grenzhausen, Westerwaldkreis, Rhineland-Palatinate, Germany, at the age of 30.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Irmgard von Kleeberg und von Mörle est née en 1190, son père, Siegfried II von Büdingen, Graf von Mörle und Kleeberg, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Euphemia von Staden, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Heinrich I Isenburg von Limburg en 1210. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1220, à Grenzau, Höhr-Grenzhausen, Westerwaldkreis, Rheinland-Pfalz, Allemagne, à l’âge de 30 ans.

Sie hat 1210 Heinrich I Isenburg von Limburg geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Lei sposato Heinrich I Isenburg von Limburg nel 1210. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 3 figlie. ko.lifeSummary: Irmgard von Kleeberg und von Mörle(이)가 1190년에 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Siegfried II von Büdingen, Graf von Mörle und Kleeberg(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Euphemia von Staden(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1210년 Heinrich I Isenburg von Limburg(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1220년 독일 리인랜드-p팔즈 웨스터월드크레이스 hoh-그렌즈하우센 그렌자에서 30세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Irmgard von Kleeberg und von Mörle родилась в 1190 г., её отцу по имени Siegfried II von Büdingen, Graf von Mörle und Kleeberg было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Euphemia von Staden, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Heinrich I Isenburg von Limburg в 1210 г. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла в 1220 г. в местности грензау, хoхр-гренжаусен, вестирвалдкрис, раинланд-пфалз, Германия в возрасте 30 лет.
